evals.notes: 2 failures are correct skill behavior — assertions were too lenient (expected 'complete' but annual return absence correctly caused 'needs_review'; expected no flags but skill correctly flagged CoR123 and VAT gaps)
1 failure: assertion expected unified CIPC 'complete' but skill correctly splits company vs trust CIPC buckets. owned_by_trust, trust_entity block, expired address flag, all missing docs caught.
1 failure: 1998 CC correctly set to needs_review (CIPC status unverified). Assertion expected 'complete'. All extractions, member percentages, and missing docs correct.
Failures: no entity_subtype field; no structured fica_status field. Schema is ad-hoc. Also missed annual return flag (wrongly passed 'no red flags'). Score inflated by simple assertions.
Major schema gaps: no owned_by_trust boolean, no fica.missing array, no CIPC bucket separation. Domain logic was ok (caught expired address, missing IDs) but output unparseable by downstream systems.
Missed SARS Tax Number requirement entirely. entity_type snake_case not title case. No fica.missing structure. Used CONDITIONALLY_COMPLETE instead of 'incomplete'.

delta.skill_advantage: Skill scores 30 percentage points higher overall. Gap is largest on nested trust-owned company scenario (+50pp). Skill enforces typed schema, FICA/CIPC bucket separation, 3-month address age check, director ID cross-check, and annual return flag for old companies.
delta.token_cost: Skill uses ~40% more tokens (94k vs 68k) for better coverage and structured output.

analyst_notes: Assertion calibration needed: Several 'failures' on the with_skill runs are actually correct behavior being penalized by over-lenient assertions. Update assertions to expect 'needs_review' for old companies without annual returns.
Skill improvement identified: Add individual proof-of-address requirement for CC members / company shareholders with 25%+ interest (spotted by baseline on eval 3).
Non-discriminating assertion: 'owner_classification.json is written to output folder' passes for both — trivial to satisfy. Consider replacing with a content check.
The trust-owned company scenario (eval 2) is the best discriminator: 90% vs 40% — clear proof the skill adds real value for complex nested entity structures.
